channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated by AI

item.title: 🇸🇳 Senegal President sacks PM Sonko, dissolves government
item.description: President Bassirou Diomaye Faye dismissed Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko and dissolved the government after months of tensions, deepening political uncertainty in the West African nation.

item.title: 🦠 International Contact Group urges DRC conflict parties to allow Ebola response
item.description: The International Contact Group for the Great Lakes raised concerns about continued conflict in eastern DRC hampering Ebola response efforts, as the outbreak continues to spread.

item.title: 🌐 INTERPOL arrests 201 suspects in largest cybercrime operation
item.description: INTERPOL conducted its largest cybercrime operation to date, targeting phishing, malware, and online scams across the Middle East and North Africa, resulting in 201 arrests.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian drone attack sparks fire at Russian oil depot in Novorossiysk
item.description: A fire broke out at an oil depot in the southern Russian port of Novorossiysk following a Ukrainian drone attack, marking another strike on Russian energy infrastructure.

item.title: 🇧🇴 Bolivian police clash with protesters in anti-government marches
item.description: Bolivian riot police clashed with anti-government protesters in La Paz for the second time in a week, as unions and opposition groups demand President Luis Arce resign.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇹🇼 US Arms Sales to Taiwan Unrelated to Iran War, Source Says
item.description: A source familiar with the matter clarified that US arms sales to Taiwan take years to process and are unrelated to the ongoing conflict with Iran, following a senior US official suggesting there was a pause due to needing weapons for the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇳 Rubio Arrives in India to Repair Strained US Ties
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived in India on Saturday to shore up a partnership battered by President Trump's tariffs and Washington's renewed engagement with India's rivals Pakistan and China.

item.title: 🇨🇭🇪🇺⚔️ Switzerland Expands Sanctions Against Russia and Belarus
item.description: Switzerland adopted parts of the European Union's latest sanctions package against Russia and Belarus over Moscow's war in Ukraine, expanding its sanctions lists in line with EU measures.

item.title: 🇦🇪🛢️ UAE Considered Leaving OPEC for Three Years Before Exit
item.description: The UAE's decision to leave OPEC was three years in the making and based on its view the world is near the "autumn of the hydrocarbon age", meaning the country needs to maximise oil revenues while it can, a senior presidential adviser said.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ Trump Official Pushed Plan to Ban Half of US Voting Machines
item.description: White House adviser Kurt Olsen, a lawyer tasked with proving election-rigging conspiracy theories, pushed a plan to target Dominion Voting Systems machines, according to a Reuters report.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Analysis: Three months in, is Trump losing the Iran war?
item.description: Despite winning nearly every battle against Iran, Trump faces growing doubts about whether he can translate tactical successes into a geopolitical win, with Iran still controlling the Strait of Hormuz and resisting nuclear concessions.

item.title: 🛍️ Shein to Buy Apparel Retailer Everlane
item.description: Chinese fast-fashion giant Shein has agreed to buy apparel retailer Everlane, expanding its presence in the US retail market.

item.title: 🇮🇩🇷🇺 Indonesia Working on Russian Oil Import Scheme
item.description: Indonesia is developing a special import scheme and regulatory framework to fulfill its plan to import 150 million barrels of crude from Russia this year, with data showing only one shipment has reached the country so far.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Attacked Russian Oil Refinery in Yaroslavl
item.description: Ukrainian forces attacked a Russian oil refinery in Yaroslavl, some 700 km from the Ukrainian border, the latest in a series of strikes by Kyiv on oil facilities in Russia.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Coal Mine Explosion Kills at Least 90
item.description: A gas explosion at a coal mine in northern China's Shanxi province killed at least 90 people, with nine still missing, making it the country's deadliest mining disaster in over a decade.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇹🇼 Taiwan's Whiplash: Trump's Blunt Warning Collides with Historic Call
item.description: President Trump's stark warning to Taiwan about paying for its defense has sent shockwaves through the island, even as he considers a historic call with Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te about the island's defenses.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇦🇪 Trump's Green Card Plan: Foreigners Must Go Home First
item.description: President Trump is forcing foreigners seeking US green cards to return to their home countries first under a new policy that critics say will dramatically reduce legal immigration.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇦🇪 Israel Thrusts Reluctant UAE into Open Over Defense Ties
item.description: Israel has pushed the UAE into a difficult position over defense ties, with the Gulf state caught between its normalization agreement with Israel and regional pressures over the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇸🇮 Slovenia's Parliament Approves Right-Wing Janez Jansa as PM
item.description: Slovenia's parliament approved right-wing veteran Janez Jansa as prime minister, returning the former EU critic to power amid concerns about the country's democratic trajectory.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Canada Alberta Referendum Vote Starts Friday
item.description: Alberta's referendum vote on whether to separate from Canada begins Friday, with polls opening for a historic vote that could reshape the country's political landscape.

item.title: 🇲🇽🇪🇺 Mexico-EU Launch New Chapter in Relations with Global Agreement
item.description: Mexico and the European Union have launched a new chapter in their relationship with a modern global agreement that deepens trade, political, and cooperation ties.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Extends Ebola Travel Ban to Green Card Holders
item.description: The US temporarily banned entry of lawful permanent residents who have been in DRC, Uganda, or South Sudan in the previous 21 days, citing Ebola concerns.

item.title: 🇨🇩 WHO Raises Ebola Risk to 'Very High' in DRC
item.description: The World Health Organization raised the risk of the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola turning into a national outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo to "very high" as the outbreak continues to spread.

item.title: 🇨🇩 DRC Bans Funeral Wakes to Contain Ebola Spread
item.description: The eastern DRC province most affected by Ebola banned funeral wakes as authorities struggle to contain the outbreak that has killed over 130 people.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 Russia War Losses Making It 'Reckless and Dangerous', NATO Warned
item.description: War losses are making Russia "reckless and dangerous", NATO has been warned, as the UK signals a defence spending boost to counter growing threats.
